title: AUC-first greedy rank stacking across heterogeneous experts
summary: ROC AUC is a ranking metric, so the biggest ensemble gains usually come from combining diverse rankers rather than re-calibrating one probability scale.
rationale: ROC AUC is a ranking metric, so the biggest ensemble gains usually come from combining diverse rankers rather than re-calibrating one probability scale. Open stacking literature shows greedy weighting is more stable than linear stacking when base predictions are highly correlated, which is likely here.
implementation_hint: Save OOF predictions from structurally different models only: baseline tabular tree, hazard/auxiliary model, latent-state variant, and any regime expert. Convert each fold’s predictions to ranks or within-race percentiles, then fit a simple positive-weight greedy stack on OOF AUROC. Prefer low-correlation members over many similar boosters.
expected_effect: Small but plausible AUROC lift and better robustness than probability averaging of near-duplicate models.
risk: If all base learners are minor variations of the same tree model, stack gains will vanish; leakage in OOF generation will produce fake CV improvements.
